output:
| Field | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Target (legal entity) | ANSYS, Inc. | Proxy/prospectus, Notice of Special Meeting, p. 2 |
| Acquirer (legal entity) | Synopsys, Inc. | Proxy/prospectus, Notice of Special Meeting, p. 2 |
| Announcement date | January 16, 2024 | Proxy/prospectus, “Background of the Merger,” p. 83 |
| Closing date (or expected closing) | Expected to be completed in the first half of 2025 | Proxy/prospectus, “Conditions to the Completion of the Merger,” p. 5; Q&A, p. 24 |
| Total enterprise value (EV) | Approximately $35 billion | Synopsys/Ansys announcement, “Transaction Terms,” Jan. 16, 2024 |
| Equity consideration | Mix of cash and stock: $197.00 in cash plus 0.3450 shares of Synopsys common stock per Ansys share; implied value of $390.19 per share based on Synopsys’ Dec. 21, 2023 closing price | Proxy/prospectus, cover letter; “Merger Consideration,” pp. 2-3 |
| Implied premium to undisturbed share price | Approximately 29% premium to Ansys’ closing price on Dec. 21, 2023; approximately 35% premium to Ansys’ 60-day VWAP ending Dec. 21, 2023 | Synopsys/Ansys announcement, “Transaction Terms,” Jan. 16, 2024 |
| Financial advisor to acquirer | Evercore Group L.L.C. | Proxy/prospectus, “Background of the Merger,” p. 69 |
| Financial advisor to target | Qatalyst Partners LP | Proxy/prospectus, “Background of the Merger,” p. 67; “Opinion of Qatalyst Partners,” p. 87 |
| Legal advisor to target | Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP; Goodwin Procter LLP | Proxy/prospectus, “Background of the Merger,” pp. 67, 69 |
| Key conditions to closing (top 3) | Ansys stockholder approval; required regulatory approvals/clearances including HSR and specified foreign antitrust/foreign investment approvals; no governmental order/legal restraint making the merger illegal or preventing closing | Proxy/prospectus, “Conditions to the Completion of the Merger,” pp. 114-115 |
| Termination fee payable by target | $950 million payable by Ansys to Synopsys in specified circumstances | Proxy/prospectus, “Termination Fees,” pp. 36, 119 |
| Termination fee payable by acquirer | $1.5 billion reverse termination fee payable by Synopsys to Ansys in specified circumstances | Proxy/prospectus, “Termination Fees,” pp. 36-37, 119 |

| Field | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Target (legal entity) | Activision Blizzard, Inc. | Proxy Summary, “Parties Involved in the Merger,” p. 1; [SEC DEFM14A](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/718877/000110465922036155/tm225196-4_defm14a.htm) |
| Acquirer (legal entity) | Microsoft Corporation; merger sub: Anchorage Merger Sub Inc., wholly owned subsidiary of Microsoft | Proxy Summary, p. 1-2; [SEC DEFM14A](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/718877/000110465922036155/tm225196-4_defm14a.htm) |
| Announcement date | January 18, 2022 | “Background of the Merger,” p. 42: joint press release issued January 18, 2022 |
| Closing date (or expected closing) | Expected to complete in Microsoft’s fiscal year ending June 30, 2023 | “Anticipated Date of Completion of the Merger,” p. 28 |
| Total enterprise value (EV) | NOT STATED | Proxy states per-share cash consideration but does not state total EV; “Merger Consideration,” p. 3 / p. 75 |
| Equity consideration | Cash; $95.00 per Activision Blizzard share, without interest | “Merger Consideration,” p. 3; “Conversion of Shares,” p. 75 |
| Implied premium to undisturbed share price | Approximately 45.3% to Activision Blizzard’s closing stock price on January 14, 2022; also 50.3% to 30-trading-day VWAP ended January 14, 2022 | Letter to Stockholders; “Recommendation of Our Board of Directors and Reasons for the Merger,” p. 43 |
| Financial advisor to acquirer | Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC | “Background of the Merger,” p. 33 |
| Financial advisor to target | Allen & Company LLC | “Opinion of Activision Blizzard’s Financial Advisor,” p. 52 |
| Legal advisor to target | Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP | “Background of the Merger,” p. 32 |
| Key conditions to closing (top 3) | Activision Blizzard stockholder approval; HSR/antitrust and foreign investment approvals; absence of injunction/legal restraint prohibiting or enjoining the merger | “Conditions to the Closing of the Merger,” p. 89 |
| Termination fee payable by target | $2,270,100,000, payable by Activision Blizzard to Microsoft in specified circumstances | “Termination Fee,” p. 92 |
| Termination fee payable by acquirer | Reverse termination fee payable by Microsoft: $2.0bn / $2.5bn / $3.0bn depending on timing of termination notice | “Reverse Termination Fee,” p. 93 |

Source document: [Juniper Networks DEFM14A filed Feb. 26, 2024](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1043604/000119312524046175/d84254ddefm14a.htm)

| Field | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Target (legal entity) | Juniper Networks, Inc. | “The Merger - Parties Involved in the Merger,” p. 32 |
| Acquirer (legal entity) | Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company | “The Merger - Parties Involved in the Merger,” p. 32 |
| Announcement date | January 9, 2024 | “The Merger - Background of the Merger,” p. 49 |
| Closing date (or expected closing) | Expected late calendar year 2024 or early calendar year 2025 | “The Special Meeting - Anticipated Date of Completion of the Merger,” p. 30 |
| Total enterprise value (EV) — if stated | NOT STATED | Not stated as transaction EV in the proxy |
| Equity consideration (cash / stock / mix; per-share value) | All cash; $40.00 per share, without interest and less applicable withholding taxes | “Summary - Merger Consideration,” pp. 2-3 |
| Implied premium to undisturbed share price (if stated) | Approx. 32% over Juniper’s $30.22 closing price on January 8, 2024 | “The Merger - Recommendation of the Board of Directors and Reasons for the Merger - Premium to Market Price,” pp. 50-51 |
| Financial advisor to acquirer | Qatalyst Partners LP and J.P. Morgan Securities LLC | “The Merger - Background of the Merger,” p. 36 |
| Financial advisor to target | Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC | “Summary - Opinion of Goldman Sachs & Co.,” pp. 8-9; “The Merger - Opinion of Goldman Sachs & Co.,” p. 59 |
| Legal advisor to target | Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom LLP | “The Merger - Background of the Merger,” p. 38 |
| Key conditions to closing (top 3) | 1. No injunction, law or order making the merger illegal or prohibiting it; 2. Juniper stockholder approval; 3. HSR waiting period expiration/termination and other required antitrust, foreign investment and regulatory approvals, without a Burdensome Condition | “Proposal 1 - Conditions to the Closing of the Merger,” pp. 91-92 |
| Termination fee payable by target (if stated) | $407,500,000 payable by Juniper under specified circumstances | “Proposal 1 - Termination Fees,” p. 94 |
| Termination fee payable by acquirer (if stated) | $815,000,000 reverse termination fee payable by HPE under specified circumstances | “Proposal 1 - Termination Fees,” p. 94 |

| Field | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Target (legal entity) | Acacia Communications, Inc. | “Parties to the Merger,” p. 37 |
| Acquirer (legal entity) | Cisco Systems, Inc. (Parent); merger effected through Amarone Acquisition Corp., a wholly owned subsidiary | “Parties to the Merger,” p. 37 |
| Announcement date | July 9, 2019 original agreement; January 14, 2021 amended and restated merger agreement | Stockholder letter, Feb. 8, 2021 |
| Closing date (or expected closing) | Expected “on or about March 1, 2021” | Q&A, “When do you expect the merger to be consummated?”, p. 20 |
| Total enterprise value (EV) | NOT STATED | Not stated in proxy summary / merger terms |
| Equity consideration | Cash; $115.00 per share of Acacia common stock | “Merger Consideration,” p. 107 |
| Implied premium to undisturbed share price | Approx. 33% premium to Acacia’s Jan. 13, 2021 closing price | Q&A, “How does the per share merger consideration compare…?”, p. 18 |
| Financial advisor to acquirer | NOT STATED | Proxy does not name an acquirer financial advisor; Parent broker representation appears in Annex A, §3.8, p. A-43 |
| Financial advisor to target | Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC | “Opinion of the Company’s Financial Advisor,” pp. 68, 73 |
| Legal advisor to target | Wilmer Cutler Pickering Hale and Dorr LLP (WilmerHale) | “Background of the Merger,” p. 39; Annex A notices, pp. A-68/A-69 |
| Key conditions to closing (top 3) | Acacia stockholder approval; no U.S. governmental order/law preventing or prohibiting the merger; HSR Act waiting periods expired or early terminated | “Conditions to the Merger,” p. 127 |
| Termination fee payable by target | $197 million | “Termination Fee,” p. 129 |
| Termination fee payable by acquirer | None stated as payable; proxy states no fee is payable by Parent to the Company under any circumstances in connection with the merger or related transactions | “Changes to the Original Merger Agreement…,” p. 38 |
